posts.topic: How to check your boiler pressure and top it up safely
posts.facebook:
📉 Boiler pressure dropped below 1 bar? Here's how to top it up yourself:

1️⃣ Find the filling loop — usually a silver braided hose under the boiler
2️⃣ Open the valves slowly until the gauge reads 1.5 bar
3️⃣ Close the valves and restart the boiler

If it drops again within a few weeks, that's a sign of a leak or internal fault — worth getting checked before it becomes a bigger problem.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
posts.instagram:
Boiler pressure dropped? Here's how to fix it yourself 📉

Step 1: Find the filling loop — usually a silver braided hose underneath the boiler
Step 2: Open both valves slowly until the gauge reads 1.5 bar
Step 3: Close the valves and restart the boiler

If it keeps dropping every few weeks, that's not normal — it usually points to a small leak or internal fault worth getting looked at.

posts.topic: Cold radiators — how to bleed them yourself
posts.facebook:
🌡️ Got a radiator that's hot at the bottom but cold at the top? It needs bleeding.

Here's how:

1️⃣ Turn the heating off and let the radiator cool
2️⃣ Use a radiator key (or flat-head screwdriver) on the bleed valve — usually top right
3️⃣ Turn slowly anticlockwise until you hear hissing air
4️⃣ When water starts to drip, close the valve
5️⃣ Check your boiler pressure afterwards — top up if needed

If the radiator is cold all over, or the problem keeps coming back, that's a different issue worth looking into.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/radiators.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655
posts.instagram:
Radiator hot at the bottom, cold at the top? Time to bleed it 🌡️

How to bleed your radiator:

1️⃣ Heat off — let it cool first
2️⃣ Find the bleed valve (top corner) — use a radiator key
3️⃣ Turn anticlockwise until you hear hissing air escaping
4️⃣ When water appears, close the valve
5️⃣ Check boiler pressure — top up if it's dropped

If it keeps happening or the radiator is cold all over, message me — could be a different problem.

posts.topic: Suspected gas leak — what to do right now

🚫 Don't switch any lights or electrics on or off
🚫 Don't use your phone inside the property
✅ Open doors and windows
✅ Turn off the gas at the mains (the lever by your meter — turn it 90°)
✅ Get out of the property
✅ Call the National Gas Emergency Service: 0800 111 999 (free, 24/7)

Once it's been declared safe by the emergency service, I can come out to find and fix the source.

4️⃣ Partially closed isolation valve under or behind the shower

posts.topic: How to bleed a radiator
posts.facebook:
🔧 Cold spots on your radiators? Bleed them.

How to do it:
1. Turn heating on and wait 20 mins
2. Feel each radiator — cold at the top = trapped air
3. Turn heating OFF and let it cool
4. Use a radiator key to open the bleed valve (anti-clockwise)
5. Air hisses out → water appears → close the valve
6. Check boiler pressure and top up if needed

If they're still cold after bleeding, it could be sludge — power flushing fixes that.

posts.topic: How to top up boiler pressure safely
posts.facebook:
📊 Boiler pressure too low? Here's how to top it up safely.

Your boiler pressure should sit between 1–1.5 bar when cold.

1. Turn the boiler OFF and let it cool
2. Find the filling loop — usually a silver/grey braided hose under the boiler
3. Open both valves (quarter turn) until pressure reaches 1.2–1.5 bar
4. Close both valves — make sure they're fully closed
5. Turn the boiler back on

⚠️ If pressure rises above 2.5 bar, don't use the boiler — call an engineer.
⚠️ If it keeps dropping, there's a leak — call me.

posts.topic: No heating or hot water — what to check first
posts.facebook:
❄️ No heating or hot water? Check these before calling an engineer.

✅ Boiler pressure — should be between 1–1.5 bar
✅ Timer and thermostat — is it set correctly? Has a power cut reset it?
✅ Check all radiator thermostats aren't turned to zero

posts.topic: Frozen condensate pipe — how to fix it yourself
posts.facebook:
🥶 Boiler stopped working in cold weather? Check the condensate pipe.

Modern condensate boilers have a plastic pipe (usually white or grey) that exits outside the property. In freezing temperatures it can ice up and cause the boiler to lock out.

How to fix it yourself:
1. Find the pipe outside (usually near the boiler on an external wall)
2. Pour warm water (NOT boiling) along the frozen section
3. Reset the boiler once it thaws

If this keeps happening, the pipe may need insulating or rerouting.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/boiler-repair.html
Call/WhatsApp Wes: 07700 155 655

Most shower pressure problems are fixable without replacing the whole unit.

posts.topic: Central heating not working — full checklist
posts.facebook:
🏠 Central heating not working? Work through this checklist before calling.

1. Is the thermostat set above the current room temperature?
2. Is the programmer/timer set correctly?
3. Is the boiler pressure between 1–1.5 bar?
4. Is the boiler showing an error code? (look it up — most are self-explanatory)
5. Have all thermostatic radiator valves been turned to zero by mistake?
6. Is the condensate pipe frozen? (in very cold weather)
7. Has a circuit breaker tripped?

If you've checked all of these and still no heat — call me.

https://bettercallwes.co.uk/services/central-heating.html

Signs you have a leak:
🔴 Damp patches on ceilings or walls
🔴 Water stains that appear and disappear
🔴 Unexplained increase in your water bill
🔴 Sound of running water when everything is off
🔴 Boiler pressure dropping regularly

The sooner a leak is found and fixed, the less damage it causes. Don't wait.
